def: s.m. Clanging, clang (of metallic instruments), clash (of swords), clatter, &c.
s.m. The sight, or the appearance, of a beautiful woman.
v.t. To cause to glitter or dance; to flash (cf. ćamkānā).
s.f. Flash, glance, glare, glitter (cf. jhalkī).
s.m. A jump, spring; a plunge; a sally.
v.n. = jhapaṭnā; — v.t. = jhāṅpnā.
adj. & s.m. Hairy, shaggy ( = jhabuʼā, jhabrā); — a shaggy animal (as a bear, &c.).
s.m.f. (?), The sound of metallic vessels, &c. striking against one another, jingling, clanking; clash (of arms, swords, &c.).
s.f. Clinking, tinkling, ringing, rattling; clank, clink, tinkle, jingle, ring, rattle; — tingling, pricking

ety: S. झं+Prk. अकओ or आकओ = S. अ+क+कः
def: s.m. Clash, clank, crash, ring.

ety: jhanak˚ = Prk. झणक्क(इ) or झणक्के(इ), fr. S. झणं (or झणत्)+कृ
def: v.n. To tinkle, jingle, clank, clink, ring, rattle, &c.; — to be angry, be offended or vexed, to take umbrage.
s.m. A flag, banner, standard, ensign; a flagstaff; (dialec.) the male flower of maize
subs: To set up a flag or banner, to plant or fix a standard
(-ko), To expose, render infamous, defame, disgrace
v.n. To be exposed, be rendered infamous, &c.
s.f. A casual or passing acquaintance.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀥𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀡𑁆𑀟 (*dhajjaṇḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit *ध्वजदण्ड (*dhvajadaṇḍa, “flagstaff”), from ध्वज (dhvaja) + दण्ड (daṇḍa). Cognate with Bhojpuri झंडा (jhaṇḍā), Punjabi ਝੰਡਾ (jhaṇḍā) / جَھنْڈا (jhanḍā), Sindhi جھَنْڊو (jhanḍo) / झंडो (jhaṇḍo), Gujarati ઝંડો (jhaṇḍo), Marathi झेंडा (jheṇḍā), Nepali झण्डा (jhaṇḍā).

def: s.f. A small flag, a flag; a flagstaff.
s.m. (dialec.), A lock (of hair); petal (of a flower); foliage (of trees).

ety: jhanḍū+lā or ˚lnā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Having a thick or fine head of hair; having thick foliage (a tree); thick, bushy; — a man or child with a thick head of hair; a tree with thick foliage.

ety: jhanḍū+lā or ˚lnā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Having a thick or fine head of hair; having thick foliage (a tree); thick, bushy; — a man or child with a thick head of hair; a tree with thick foliage.
s.m. = jhāg, q.v. s.m. Foam, froth; scum
subs: v.n. To foam, to froth; to foam with rage.

def: s.m. An upper garment; a long frock or coat ( = jhagulā, q.v.). A child's frock or coat (reaching to the feet); a child's angarkhā.
s.f. (dialec.) = jhāṛ; jhākaṛ, q.v. s.m. A bush, shrub; bushes, bramble, brushwood, scrub, underwood, brake; a kind of arabesque work; a lustre or chandelier; a kind of firework
subs: s.m. Brambles, underwood; a tangled thicket, a brake
adj. (f. -ī), Like a bush or shrub; (in Bot.) cymose
s.m. Bush-thorn, bramble-thorn; (met.) a quarrelsome person
adj. & s.m. Full of brambles, bushy; — a forest, jungle; the forest of Baijnāth
v.n. To stick or cling (to one) like a bramble; to stick close (to one)

ety: Prk. अज्झंगिआ S. अध्यङ्गिका; see jhaṅgā
def: s.f. = jhingaliyā, q.v. s.f. A child's upper garment; a small frock or coat, a tunic (see next).
s.f. = jhāʼīṅ, q.v. s.f. Shadow; reflection; a dusky oolour; a dark spot in a looking-glass (where the quick-silver has worn away); a freckle, a black spot on the face (not a mole)
subs: s.f. A children's game (in which they go round in a circle and make a noise like the cawing of crows).

def: s.f. A swoon, faint, stupefaction.
s.f. = jhāṅjh, q.v. s.f. Anger, passion, pet, rage; dispute, quarrel; impatience (of hunger, &c., or in a horse expecting his corn)
subs: v.n. To get into a rage, to rage, fume; to dispute, quarrel.

def: s.f. = jhāṅjhan, q.v. s.f. A tinkling anklet ( = jhāṅjh).
s.m. = jhanjhaṭ, q.v. s.f. Wrangling, squabbling, quarrel.
s.f. Anger, passion, pet, rage; dispute, quarrel; impatience (of hunger, &c., or in a horse expecting his corn)
subs: v.n. To get into a rage, to rage, fume; to dispute, quarrel.

def: s.f. A cymbal; a hollow tinkling anklet.
s.f. = jhāṅjh, q.v. s.f. Anger, passion, pet, rage; dispute, quarrel; impatience (of hunger, &c., or in a horse expecting his corn)
subs: v.n. To get into a rage, to rage, fume; to dispute, quarrel.

def: s.m. A perforated ladle, a strainer or slice; — a kind of worm or caterpillar, cabbage-worm.
s.f. Wind, wind and heavy rain, a squall, gale, hurricane; — a clang or sharp clanking sound, jingling, rattling
subs: (jhanjhā+anil), s.m. Violent wind and rain, a high wind in the rainy season, a typhoon, or the sort of tempest frequent during the south-west monsoon
s.m. Wind with rain, a storm, squall.

def: s.f. Difficulty, dilemma, perplexity, trouble, vexation.
v.n. (dialec.) = jhunjh-lānā.
v.t. To shake to and fro, to swing, vibrate (as dyers do cloth in order to dry it; cf. jhalnā).
s.f. A tinkling anklet ( = jhāṅjh).
s.m. The sound of metallic vessels, &c. striking together, clang, clank, jingle, tinkle, rattle, ring, &c.: = jhan-jhan karnā, v.n. To clang, clank, jingle, &c. ( = jhanjhanānā).
adj. (f. -ī), Irritable, petulant, peevish, fretful.

ety: fr. jhanjhanānā; for āhaṭ, see jhunjhlāhaṭ
def: s.f. Jingling, tinkling, tinkle, rattling, &c. ( = jhan-jhan; jhan-kār); — petulance, peevishness.

ety: S. झञ्झन; or Prk. झणझण˚ = S. ध्वनि redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v n. To clang, jingle, tinkle, clink, ring, rattle; to fly into a passion, to rage, storm, be petulant or peevish; to tingle, be benumbed or cramped, go to sleep (as the limbs).
s.f. Tingling, numbness (of the limbs), the sensation as if pins and needles were striking into the limbs.
adj. Perforated, full of holes, riddled; cracked, broken, leaky; — s.f. = jhājhar, jhāṅjh, q.v.
s.m. = jhajjhar, q.v. A large porous water-jar; a porous goglet ( = jhanjhar).
s.f. Wrangling, squabbling, quarrel.
s.m.f.(?) Wrangling, contention; — perplexity, dilemma; encumbrance
subs: (-se), To get up a quarrel (with), to wrangle.

def: adj. Intricate, difficult, perplexing.
adj. & s.m. Contentious, quarrelsome; — a wrangler, a quarrelsome fellow.

s.f. A perforated pot or vessel; — a kind of play, a procession of girls at night (in the month Asin) carrying on their heads perforated earthen pots with lamps inside, in honour of Queen Sānjī; a kind of song (prob. that sung by the children in the procession described above).
s.f. Tingling, &c. (cf. jhanjhanī).
adj. Petulant, passionate, wrathful, furious.
s.f. A small cymbal.
s.f. Clinking, tinkling, ringing; humming, buzzing; burring (of a grasshopper); chirping (of a cricket, &c.).
v.t. To pull and gnaw, rend and tear (as a dog, &c. does a bone, flesh, or another animal), to shake, to worry; to grapple with.

adj. & s.f. = jhajhrī, q.v. adj. Perforated, full of holes, riddled; porous; reticulated; grated; latticed, trellised; — s.f. (S. jarjarīkā), A porous goglet; a grating; a lattice, a screen ( = jhanjhrī).

s.f. = jhāṅjhī, q.v. s.f. A perforated pot or vessel; — a kind of play, a procession of girls at night (in the month Asin) carrying on their heads perforated earthen pots with lamps inside, in honour of Queen Sānjī; a kind of song (prob. that sung by the children in the procession described above).

s.f. = jhanjhī, q.v.; — झिंजी jhinjī, s.f. = jhinjhī, q.v. s.f. A perforated pot or vessel; — a kind of play, a procession of girls at night (in the month Asin) carrying on their heads perforated earthen pots with lamps inside, in honour of Queen Sānjī; a kind of song (prob. that sung by the children in the procession described above).
s.m.(?) A species of ser in the lower Doab.
v.t. = jhanjhoṛnā, q.v. v.t. To pull and gnaw, rend and tear (as a dog, &c. does a bone, flesh, or another animal), to shake, to worry; to grapple with.
s.f. Peeping, spying; a peep

ety: i.q. jhoṅk; jḥoṅkā; see jhoṅknā, 'to throw'
def: s.m. A herd (of deer); a flock (of birds); a cloud or host (of locusts), &c.
v.t. = jhan-kārnā, q.v. v.t. To cause to give forth a clinking sound to tinkle, jingle, clank, clink, ring, rattle, &c.
v.t. To cause to peep, to make or to get (one) to peep or spy, &c.

ety: jhāṅk, q.v.+ar = Prk. अडो = S. अ+रः
def: s.m. Bush, bramble, underwood.
s.f. A low murmuring sound, humming, buzzing; burring (of a grasshopper); chirping (of a cricket), &c.; — clinking, tinkling, &c. = jhanak, q.v.
s.m. = jhaṅkhāṛ, q.v. s.m. A tree with bare branches, a leafless tree; — jhaṅkhāṛā, s.m. A stag; an elk ( = jhāṅkh; so called from the resemblance of its horns to a leafless tree).
s.m. = jhaṅkharā; jhaṅkhāṛ, q.v. s.m. A tree with bare branches, a leafless tree; — jhaṅkhāṛā, s.m. A stag; an elk ( = jhāṅkh; so called from the resemblance of its horns to a leafless tree).
v.t. To cause to give forth a clinking sound to tinkle, jingle, clank, clink, ring, rattle, &c.
v.n. To murmur, hum, buzz (as bees); to burr (as a grasshopper); to chirp (as a cricket), &c. ( = jhingārnā).
s.m. A stag; an elk; a deer.
s.m. A tree with bare branches, a leafless tree; — jhaṅkhāṛā, s.m. A stag; an elk ( = jhāṅkh; so called from the resemblance of its horns to a leafless tree).
s.m. A tree with bare branches, a leafless tree; — jhaṅkhāṛā, s.m. A stag; an elk ( = jhāṅkh; so called from the resemblance of its horns to a leafless tree).
s.m. = jhaṅkhāṛ, q.v. s.m. A tree with bare branches, a leafless tree; — jhaṅkhāṛā, s.m. A stag; an elk ( = jhāṅkh; so called from the resemblance of its horns to a leafless tree).

ety: S. झं+कृतः; or rt. of jhaṅkh-nā+S. इतः
def: part. adj. Shaken, struck (by the wind, &c.), blasted; horror-struck, having the hair standing on end, terrified; — raving; lamenting.
v.n. To be shaken, to be struck or blasted; to be horror-struck, to have the hair standing on end, to be terrified, be scared; to shudder.
v.n. = jhakhnā = jhaknā, q.v. v.n. To chatter, &c. = jhaknā, q.v. v.n. To babble, chatter, prate, talk nonsense, to rave; to lament; to rail (see jhak mārnā).
v.n. (Braj.) = jhaṅkhnā, jhaknā, q.v. v.n. To be shaken, to be struck or blasted; to be horror-struck, to have the hair standing on end, to be terrified, be scared; to shudder.

ety: rt. of jhāṅknā+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A peep-show, a show, exhibition; a scene (in a play).

ety: jhāṅk˚ = Prk. अज्झख्व(इ), fr. S. अध्यक्ष
def: v.n. To peep (into, -meṅ, or at), to spy, to look (through a hole or opening); to put (one's) head out (of a door or window); to inspect, to examine narrowly (into); to look (in for a short time), to pay a flying visit (to one).
v.t. = jhakornā, q.v. v.t. To shake, to put in motion; to drive or beat (as wind or rain); cf. jhakolnā.
v.n. To tingle; to become benumbed or cramped (the legs, feet, &c.), to feel the sensation of pins and needles striking into the limbs.
s.m. A frame of bamboo covered with matting (used as a door or screen), a matted shutter; an enclosure of bamboo-work for fowls, &c.
s.m. A large jhāṅp or matted shutter (such as constitutes a village gate).

ety: jhāṅp˚ = Prk. झंप(इ) = S. क्षप्य(ते), pass. of rt. क्षप् (used actively)
def: v.t. To cover, hide, conceal, screen, shade; to shut, close.
s.f. A bad woman, a harlot; the common king-crow, Dicrurus albirietus.
s.f. (prov.) = jhanak, q.v. s.f. Clinking, tinkling, ringing, rattling; clank, clink, tinkle, jingle, ring, rattle; — tingling, pricking

ety: rt. of jhāṅs-nā+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. Wheedling, flattery, humbug, hoax, trick, deception; eager desire, inclination; design; — an assessment made by general estimate (without specific ground); — jhāṅse-bāz, s.m. A wheedler, &c. = jhāṅsū, q.v.
subs: jhāṅsā denā (-ko), To wheedle, delude, trick, deceive, &c. = jhāṅsnā, q.v.
(-ke), To be wheedled, tricked, hoaxed; to be cheated, deceived, &c.

ety: jhāṅs˚ = S. अध्यस्य(ति), rt. अस् with अधि
def: v.t. To flatter, coax, wheedle, hoax, humbug; to delude, deceive; to corrupt, seduce, debauch; — to desire eagerly, to long for, hanker after, to covet.

ety: rt. of jhāṅsnā+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. Flatterer, coaxer, wheedler, humbug; deceiver, cheat; corrupter, seducer.

ety: S. झाटा, rt. झट्; cf. जटा
def: s.f. The hair of the private parts, pubes.
s.m. = jhāmā, q.v. s.m. Pumice stone; vitrified brick (com. used to clean the hands and feet with; also written jhāṅwāṅ).
s.m. = jhāmā, q.v. s.m. Pumice stone; vitrified brick (com. used to clean the hands and feet with; also written jhāṅwāṅ).
s.m. A kind of rice.

ety: jhāṅwā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To clean the feet by rubbing them with jhāmā or jhaṅwāṅ; — v.n. To tan (in the sun), become dusky or black; to wither, fade, become faint; to smoulder; to be extinguished (a fire)
s.f. A tinkling anklet ( = jhāṅjh).

ety: jhāṅw˚ = jhāṅp˚, rt. of jhāṅpnā, q.v.+lī = Prk. डिआ = S. र or ल+इका
def: s.f. A look or glance with partly-closed eyes, a side look; a wink; an amorous glance, ogling, coquetry
subs: (-ko), To wink (at), cast amorous glances (at), to ogls.

def: s.f. Hot wind.
adj. (f. -ī), Dark-coloured, dusky, black.
s.f. = jhāṅwar.
s.m. Spring; dart; jerk; — quickness, rapidity; — adj. Quick; brisk; — adv. Quickly, in a trice
subs: v.n. To overset (as a paper kite in flying).

def: s.m. A narrow-mouthed basket.

ety: jhap, q.v.+Prk. अकं or आकं, and अकअं, &c.; S. अ+कं and क+कं
def: adj. Quick, &c.
subs: jhapāke-se, adv. Quickly, with all speed, rapidly, post-haste, in a trice.

def: (v.n. of jhapaknā, q.v.), s.f.m.(?) Moving to and fro (a fan, &c.), fanning; swaying, swinging, waving; winking, blinking, wink, blink; nod; a blast, gust (of wind).

ety: jhap, q.v.+Prk. अकं or आकं, and अकअं, &c.; S. अ+कं and क+कं
def: adj. Quick, &c.
subs: jhapāke-se, adv. Quickly, with all speed, rapidly, post-haste, in a trice.

ety: jhapak˚ = Prk. झंपक्क(इ) or झंपक्के(इ), fr. S. झंप+कृ
def: v.t. To move to and fro (a fan, &c.), to swing, wave; to fan ( = jhalnā); to throw on; — v.n. To lower or drop (the eye-lids, through drowsiness, fear, or shame); to close (the eyes), to nap, doze; to be or feel ashamed or afraid (of, -se); to wink, to blink, to twinkle; — to pounce (upon, -par), spring (at or upon); to snatch or snap (at).
v.n. To close the eyes, to take a nap, to doze.
s.f. A sharp shower; driving rain (see jhaṭās).
adj. Sharp, astute, deceitful, fraudulent.
s.f. Spring, bound, leap (as of a tiger); jerk, snatch, snap; flap, whisk; rush, dash, pounce, onset, onslaught, assault; a round (in a fight); &c. (see next).
s.f. Quickness, &c. = jhapāk, q.v. adj. Quick, &c.
subs: jhapāke-se, adv. Quickly, with all speed, rapidly, post-haste, in a trice.

def: s.f. Quickness, &c. = jhapāk, q.v. adj. Quick, &c.
subs: jhapāke-se, adv. Quickly, with all speed, rapidly, post-haste, in a trice.

def: s.m. = jhapāṭ; jhapākā; and jhapaṭṭā, qq.v. s.f. Quickness, &c. = jhapāk, q.v. adj. Quick, &c.
subs: jhapāke-se, adv. Quickly, with all speed, rapidly, post-haste, in a trice.

def: v.n. To go quickly, run with all speed; to run or fly (at, -par), to dash (at), to make a sudden attack (on); to spring, pounce, or swoop down (upon); to snatch or snap (at)
subs: v.t. To seize and carry off; to spring or pounce upon; to snatch or snap up.

def: s.m. Spring; rush, &c. ( = jhapaṭ, q.v.); — a sustained effort or act; a spurt or go; a stretch
subs: (as of swords); swoop, stoop (as of a bird of prey); desolating sweep (as of an epidemic); vehemence, violence, fury (as of fever, wind, rain, or fire); gust (of wind): shock, collision; influence, blast (of an evil spirit)
(-par), To pounce (upon), make a dash or rush (at), to swoop down (upon); to snatch (at), &c., see jhapaṭnā
(-ke), To fall into the clutches (of); to come under the influence (of), be blasted (by an evil spirit), to be possessed, be bewitched, &c.

def: s.f. = jhapaṭ, and jhapaṭṭā, q.v.
v.n. = jhapaṭnā, q.v. v.n. To go quickly, run with all speed; to run or fly (at, -par), to dash (at), to make a sudden attack (on); to spring, pounce, or swoop down (upon); to snatch or snap (at)
subs: v.t. To seize and carry off; to spring or pounce upon; to snatch or snap up.

def: s.m. = jhaṭkā; jhapkī; jhoṅkā, qq.v. s.m. A jerk, pull, wrench, shake; a twitch, a stitch; a gust (of wind)
subs: v.t. To jerk, to pull with a jerk; to jolt, shake.

def: v.n. = jhapaknā, q.v. v.t. To move to and fro (a fan, &c.), to swing, wave; to fan ( = jhalnā); to throw on; — v.n. To lower or drop (the eye-lids, through drowsiness, fear, or shame); to close (the eyes), to nap, doze; to be or feel ashamed or afraid (of, -se); to wink, to blink, to twinkle; — to pounce (upon, -par), spring (at or upon); to snatch or snap (at).
s.f. A spring (as of a tiger); a snatch, snap; drowsiness, nodding, a nap or doze; a wink, blink, twinkle
subs: v.n. To take a nap, to doze.

ety: jhap˚ = S. क्षपय-, rt. क्षि, cf. ćhap
def: v.t. To wash, to rinse.
s.m. A kind of sedan chair (used in the hills), a 'jompon.'
v.t. To cause to run or go quickly, to despatch with all speed.
s.m. A bush, shrub; bushes, bramble, brushwood, scrub, underwood, brake; a kind of arabesque work; a lustre or chandelier; a kind of firework
subs: s.m. Brambles, underwood; a tangled thicket, a brake
adj. (f. -ī), Like a bush or shrub; (in Bot.) cymose
s.m. Bush-thorn, bramble-thorn; (met.) a quarrelsome person
adj. & s.m. Full of brambles, bushy; — a forest, jungle; the forest of Baijnāth
v.n. To stick or cling (to one) like a bramble; to stick close (to one)

def: s.f. = jhaṛ, q.v. s.m. A bush, shrub; bushes, bramble, brushwood, scrub, underwood, brake; a kind of arabesque work; a lustre or chandelier; a kind of firework
subs: s.m. Brambles, underwood; a tangled thicket, a brake
adj. (f. -ī), Like a bush or shrub; (in Bot.) cymose
s.m. Bush-thorn, bramble-thorn; (met.) a quarrelsome person
adj. & s.m. Full of brambles, bushy; — a forest, jungle; the forest of Baijnāth
v.n. To stick or cling (to one) like a bramble; to stick close (to one)

def: s.f. The heat (from a fire).
s.m. The sound produced in tearing cloth; a tatter, a shred
subs: v.t. To tear to shreds or ribands.

ety: S. क्षर, or rt. of jhaṛnā
def: s.f. A falling, fall (of leaves, &c., e.g. pat-jhaṛ); a shower, downpour, heavy or continuous rain ( = jhaṛī); a volley; — the tongue or bolt of a lock
subs: adv. In showers; heavily, incessantly, continuously (raining)
s.f. A storm of wind and rain, a squall.

def: s.f. Sweeping, cleaning, &c. (see jhāṛ-nā); a purge; a shower, continuous and heavy rain, downpour ( = jhaṛī)
subs: adv. Sweeping clean out, making a clean sweep, entirely
s.f. Clearing off (an account, &c.), full payment; finishing touch or stroke
v.n. To shower, to pour down, to rain without ceasing
s.f. Juggling, conjuring, exorcising; incantation, sorcery, hocus-pocus (particularly to cure a disease or the bite of a snake)
s.f. Sweeping, dusting, &c. ( = jhāṛ-poṅćh); sweepings; perquisites.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = jhājhā, q.v. s.m. An intoxicating mixture made of bhaṅg or hemp ( = gāṅjā).
s.m. A kind of sieve; — (jhāra(nā)+S. कः), Drizzling rain, drizzle (syn. jhaṛī; jhīsī).
s.m. A bush, &c. = jhāṛ (used chiefly in comp.)
subs: jhāṛe-jhapṭe jānā, jhāṛā-jhaṭkā phirnā, v.n. lit. 'To walk among the bushes'; to go to the necessary, to go to the rear, to ease oneself.

ety: rt. of jhāṛnā+ā = Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. Sweeping, cleaning, clearing; minute search (made by making a person suspected of stealing take off all his clothes); sweepings; a stool, purge, evacuation; excrement, dirt, filth; — conjuring, hocus-pocus, incantation
subs: (-ko), To submit to minute search (of one's person); to hocus-pocus, conjure, mutter incantations (over)
s.m. Sweepings of a (druggist's) shop; compound medicine.

def: adv. Making a clean sweep, wholly, altogether.
s.m. Quickness, speed, haste, hurry
subs: adv. With all speed, quickly, post-haste, &c.

def: s.m. Quickness, speed, haste, hurry
subs: adv. With all speed, quickly, post-haste, &c.

def: s.m. = jharokhā, q.v. s.m. Loop-hole, eyelet-hole, lattice, window, casement, skylight; open door or arch (of a summer-house).
adv. = jhaṛā, q.v. s.m. (dialec.) = jhājhā, q.v. s.m. An intoxicating mixture made of bhaṅg or hemp ( = gāṅjā).

s.f. The falling off (of fruit from a tree, or of the hair of a man or animal, or of the feathers of a bird); a shower (of fruit, &c.); shavings, parings, sweepings, rubbish; small profits or savings; pickings, perquisites; snuff (of a candle).
(see next), Sweepings, rubbish ( = jhaṛan); — a coarse cloth for wiping furniture, &c., a duster, a dish-clout.
v.t. To cause to be swept, to get (a place) swept; to get (a cloth, &c.) shaken or dusted; to cause to be exorcised.
adv. See s.v. jhāṛ, 'sweeping.' s.m. A bush, shrub; bushes, bramble, brushwood, scrub, underwood, brake; a kind of arabesque work; a lustre or chandelier; a kind of firework
subs: s.m. Brambles, underwood; a tangled thicket, a brake
adj. (f. -ī), Like a bush or shrub; (in Bot.) cymose
s.m. Bush-thorn, bramble-thorn; (met.) a quarrelsome person
adj. & s.m. Full of brambles, bushy; — a forest, jungle; the forest of Baijnāth
v.n. To stick or cling (to one) like a bramble; to stick close (to one)

def: s.f. Heat, fieriness, pungency (as of pepper, &c.); flame, blaze; acrimony; contention, sparring, fight, cock-fight, bird-fight; — being possessed of a devil
subs: s.f. Contention, sparring, fighting (particularly of birds).

def: v.n. = jhaṛapnā, q.v.
v.n. To spar, to fight (as cocks, &c.), to contend.
s.m. = jhaṛotā, q.v. s.m. The end of the season (of fruit, &c.); — adj. Going out of season.
v.n. = jhar-barnā; and laṛ-baṛānā, qq.v. v.n. To burn furiously, to be all in flames, to be entirely burnt.
v.n. To burn furiously, to be all in flames, to be entirely burnt.
v.n. = jhar-barnā, q.v. v.n. To burn furiously, to be all in flames, to be entirely burnt.
s.m. = jhaṛ-ber, q.v.
s.m. = jhaṛ-ber, q.v.
s.m. The fruit of the wild jujube tree or Zizyphus jujuba (see next).
s.f. = jhaṛ-berī, q.v.
[S. झटि+वदर+इका A wild ber or jujube bush, Zizyphus jujuba; the wild lote-tree.
s.f. = jhāṛī, q.v. s.f. A pitcher with a long neck and a spout to it, an ewer.
s.f. A shrub, bush; bramble, brushwood, small bushes, underwood; copse, brake, thicket; wood, forest, jungle (see jhāṛ); — a jasmine bush or creeper

ety: rt. of jhaṛnā+Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Continued rain, showers; pickings, perquisites
s.f. A pitcher with a long neck and a spout to it, an ewer.
s.f. A waterfall, cascade.
s.m. = jhajjhar, q.v. A large porous water-jar; a porous goglet ( = jhanjhar).
v.t. To shake, jerk, flap.
s.f. Shaking (of a tree, &c.), flapping (of wings).

ety: jhār˚ = Prk. झाड(इ) or झाडे(इ) = S. शादय(ति), caus. of rt. शद्
def: v.t. To sift; to winnow (cf. jhāṛnā).

ety: jhāṛ˚ = Prk. अज्झड्डे(इ) = S. अध्यर्दय(ति), caus. of rt. अर्द् with अधि
def: v.t. To clean, to sweep, to brush, to dust
subs: v.t. To sweep up, to clean
v.t. To look closely into, to search thoroughly, to examine minutely, to scrutinize
v.t. (intens.) To brush, brusn off, clean up, clear out, sweep away, sweep clean
v.t. (intens.) To sweep out, sweep away; to dust or clean well, &c.

ety: caus. of jhaṛnā; — jhāṛ˚ = Prk. झाड(इ) or झाडे(इ) = S. शादय(ति), rt. शद्
def: v.t. To cause to drop or fall; to strain; to shake (trees, carpets, &c.), to beat (bushes, &c.); to shake off, to cast (its feathers, as a bird); to brandish or flourish (a sword, stick, &c.), to make a cut (at); to give one a shaking or a set-down; to knock off, break off; to dash, strike, flap; to shed sparks, to strike out fire (as from a flint); to conjure, hocus-pocus, to repeat spells or charms over
subs: v.t. To exorcise, to repeat spells or charms over (a sick person).

def: v.n. = جهڙنا jhaṛnā, q.v. v.t. To sift; to winnow (cf. jhāṛnā).

ety: jhaṛ˚ = Prk. झड(इ) for छड(इ) = S. शद(ति), rt. शद्
def: v.n. To drop, fall, fall off (as fruit from a tree, hair from an animal, &c.); to be shed; to be poured; to be discharged (as a volley, or semen, &c.); to be shaken (as a cloth, &c.); to be winnowed, be sifted; to shake; to be sounded or played (as the naubat); to remain over, to be saved
subs: v.n. (intens.) To fall off, to drop off, to wither, to fade.

def: v.n. To be swept, be swept off, be swept clean; to be beaten or dusted (as clothes, carpets, &c.).

v.n. To flow or run in a slender stream, to trickle; to fall in drops, to drop; to ooze; to be shaken (as a cloth, &c.); to be winnowed, be sifted; — jharnā, s.m. A spring, a cascade, a jet dʼeau; a kind of sieve; a perforated skimmer.
adj. Falling off; (in Bot.) deciduous.
s.m. = jharokhā, q.v. s.m. Loop-hole, eyelet-hole, lattice, window, casement, skylight; open door or arch (of a summer-house).
s.m. Loop-hole, eyelet-hole, lattice, window, casement, skylight; open door or arch (of a summer-house).

ety: rt. of jhaṛnā+Prk. वत्तअं or वट्टअं = S. वार्त्त+कं or वृत्त+कं
def: s.m. The end of the season (of fruit, &c.); — adj. Going out of season.

ety: rt. of jhaṛnā+Prk. वत्तअं or वट्टअं = S. वार्त्त+कं or वृत्त+कं
def: s.m. The end of the season (of fruit, &c.); — adj. Going out of season.
v.t. To make (cocks or other birds) fight; to cause to spar or fight, to set by the ears; to take possession of, to blast (as an evil spirit).
v.n. To be set a-fighting; to be set by the ears; to be possessed, be under the influence (of an evil spirit).
s.m. (dialec.) = jharnā, q.v. v.t. To sift; to winnow (cf. jhāṛnā).
s.m. The sound produced by the tearing of cloth ( = jhar).

ety: jha ṛ(nā)+tī = Prk. अत्तिआ = S. आप्तिका (आपि caus. augment+aff. ति+का)
def: Searching, &c. = jhāṛā, q.v.
subs: (-kī) = jhāṛā lenā. s.m. (dialec.) = jhājhā, q.v. s.m. An intoxicating mixture made of bhaṅg or hemp ( = gāṅjā).

ety: rt. of jhāṛnā 'to sweep,'+ū = Prk. इडओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. A broom, a besom, a whisk; — a comet
subs: v.n. To be swept, to be swept clean; to be cleaned out
v.t. To sweep, to clean; to make a clean sweep of all in a house, to spare nothing
v.t. To crush (one) down, to scold, chide; to castigate, chastise
s.m. A sweeper (by caste and occupation, syn. ḥalāl-ḵẖor, bhangī)
v.t. To beat with a broom.

def: s.m. A kind of nutritious grass (the grain resembling that of shāmāḵẖ or Panicum frumentaceum, of which it is considered to be a wild species).
v.t. To cause to be shaken or dusted, to cause to be beaten; to have (a place) swept, &c.
s.m. A fish; the sign Pisces
subs: s.m. The sign Pisces; — name of Kāmdev, the god of love.

def: s.m. An arbour, bower, place overgrown with creepers; a wood, thicket.

ety: S. झट्, perhaps for अध्यट; cf. झट्+इति
def: s.m. Quickness, celerity; — adj. Quick, brisk; — adv. Quickly, speedily, instantly, at once (cf. jhap)
s.f. Jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum.
s.f. A jerk, wrench, shake; a toss, throw; a twitch, stitch (see next).

ety: jhaṭak˚ = Prk. झटक्क(इ) or झटक्के(इ), fr. S. झट+कृ
def: v.t. To pull with a jerk, to jerk, touse, twitch; to snatch, snap, wrench; to shake (a cloth, &c.); — v.n. To be shaken, pulled down (as by sickness), to fall off (in flesh), to become lean or thin
subs: v.t. To snatch or jerk away

def: s.f. Squall, driving rain; gust; shock; clap, flap.
s.m. A small tree, a shrub, bush (see jhāṛ).
s.f. The plant Flacourtia cataphracta.
adv. See jhaṭ.
s.m. Name of a small clan of Rājpūts in the upper Doab.
s.m. A jerk, pull, wrench, shake; a twitch, a stitch; a gust (of wind)
subs: v.t. To jerk, to pull with a jerk; to jolt, shake.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀛𑀝𑁆𑀝𑁆 (jhaṭṭ) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓𑁆- (-kk-).

def: part. (of jhaṭaknā), Broken asunder, torn by a sudden jerk, torn to pieces; slaughtered by having the head cut off (in the Hindū way); — s.m. An animal so slaughtered (for sacrifice, &c., the only meat lawful for Hindus to eat).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀛𑀝𑁆𑀝𑁆 (jhaṭṭ) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓𑁆- (-kk-).

def: (caus. of jhaṭaknā), To shake, to pull, &c.
s.f. (dialec.), Fire, flame, heat.

s.m. A tree of the Tamarisk kind, Tamarix gallica, or T. Indica (called, in P., gaz, or shora-gaz, from its growing in marshy or sandy ground: it is much used for thatching, making baskets, hedging, and as fuel); — name of a plant (that grows wild, and is used to adulterate chiretta).
s.f. (dialec.) Itching
v.t. To bend the head for the purpose of butting, to butt, to gore (see jhoknā).
adj. = jhauṅr, jhauṅrā, q.v. adj. Dusky, tawny, copper-coloured, reddish-yellow, wheat-coloured, straw-coloured.

adj. Dusky, tawny, copper-coloured, reddish-yellow, wheat-coloured, straw-coloured.
adj. (f. -ī), Excessively burnt or scorched.

s.m. Squabble, &c. = jhauṛ.
v.t. To beat, thrash; to handle roughly, to bang, pommel, drub; to claw, scratch, tear.
s.m. = jhāʼū, q.v.; — a large open basket (made from the twigs of the jhāʼū).

s.f. = jhābaṛ, q.v. s.f. Low-land on which water lies; a pool: marshy land, a marsh, fen, swamp.

s.m. = jhāʼū, q.v. s.m. A tree of the Tamarisk kind, Tamarix gallica, or T. ety: fr. the intens. of S. rt. हृ 'to take'
def: v.t. To take, catch; to admit; to accept, to bear, endure, submit to, undergo, experience; to digest (prob. connected with S. jīrṇa)
subs: v.t. (intens.), To catch, grasp; to fold in the arms.

def: v.t. To blow or waft away, to brush off; to swing (a fan, &c.) gently to and fro, to fan.
s.m. A pit-fall, a pit, hole, well; a well that has fallen in or become choked.

s.f. A chink, crevice, &c. ( = jhirī, q.v.). s.f. Opening, fissure, cleft, crevice, slit, crack, chink; — a small spring dug in a nālā (where the water percolates a few feet below the surface); — blight; withered or blighted grain.
s.f. = jhijhak, q.v. s.f. A start (through astonishment, surprise, or fear), recoil; astonishment, amazement; timidity; shyness.
v.n. = jhijhaknā, q.v. v.n. To be startled; to start, shrink, wince, recoil; to boggle; to be timid; to be shy, retiring, or reserved; — to feel the sensation of the limbs being asleep or benumbed
subs: adj. & s.m. Starting, easily startled, timid; shy, retiring, reserved; — one who starts, &c.; a boggler.

wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀛𑀺𑀮𑁆𑀮 (*jhilla) (compare 𑀛𑀺𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀇 (jhillaï, “to bathe”), 𑀛𑀺𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀻 (jhillī, “wave”)), of uncertain origin.

def: s.f. The bass (in music or singing); — the treble (cf. jīl).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀛𑀺𑀮𑁆𑀮 (*jhilla) (compare 𑀛𑀺𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀇 (jhillaï, “to bathe”), 𑀛𑀺𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀻 (jhillī, “wave”)), of uncertain origin.

def: s.f. Armour, coat of mail; the visor of a helmet.

ety: Prk. झीण = S. क्षीण+अंगं, and अंग+कं
def: adj. Worn, thin, emaciated, lean; loose, ragged (cf. jhilgā); thin, fine (as cloth); — jhilaṅgā, s.m. = jhilgā, q.v.
s.m. A foot-soldier; a peon.

ety: Prk. झीण = S. क्षीण+अंगं, and अंग+कं
def: adj. Worn, thin, emaciated, lean; loose, ragged (cf. jhilgā); thin, fine (as cloth); — jhilaṅgā, s.m. = jhilgā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Old, worn, worn out, loose, ragged, in pieces or shreds; — s.m. A ćārpāʼī or bed the string or tape of which is loose and broken (cf. jhilangā).
s.m. (prov.), A band, company, troop; a flock, herd.

ety: Prk. & S. झिल्ली; prob. fr. Prk. झीण = S. क्षीण; cf. jhilgā, jhilangā
def: s.f. A thin skin, a pellicle, the inner skin (of the stomach, &c.), a membrane, the omentum or caul; the inner bark (of a tree); parchment.
s.f. A cricket.
s.m. A cricket ( = jhillī).
s.f. A cricket ( = jhillī).
s.m. Sparkling, flashing (of water, &c. in the sun or light); twinkling, twinkle (of a star, &c.), scintillation; flicker; — a venetian blind, a shutter ( = jhilmilī); a kind of gauze
subs: v.n. To sparkle, flash, &c.; see jhilmilānā.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Sparkling, flashing; gauze-like, thin, fine.

ety: jhilmil, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To sparkle, to flash (as water &c. in the sun); to twinkle, to scintillate; to undulate as the flame of a candle, or water), to flicker.
s.f. A venetian blind, a shutter; — a kind of gauze ( = jhilmil).
v.n. To be undergone or experienced, be endured or borne; to be digested.

ety: Prk. झीणं and झीणअं; S. क्षीणं and क्षीण+कं
def: adj. (f. -ī), Diminished, wasted, worn away; thin, emaciated; delicate, slender; fine; subtle (cf. jhin-jhinā).

ety: Prk. झीणं and झीणअं; S. क्षीणं and क्षीण+कं
def: adj. (f. -ī), Diminished, wasted, worn away; thin, emaciated; delicate, slender; fine; subtle (cf. jhin-jhinā).
s.f. = jhinṭī, q.v. s.f. The shrub Barleria cristata.
s.m. = jhīṅgā, q.v.
s.m. The vegetable Luffa acutaṇgula, or Cucumis acutangulus
s.f. A shrimp, a prawn (cf. ćiṅgṛī).

ety: jhinga = jhangā, q.v.+Prk. डिआ = S. र or ल+इका
def: s.f. A child's upper garment; a small frock or coat, a tunic (see next).
s.f. = jhan-kār, and ćiṅghāṛ, qq.v. s.m. Bush, bramble, underwood.
s.f. = jhiṅgār, q.v. s.m. Bush, bramble, underwood.
s.m. = jhīṅgar, q.v. s.m. Bush, bramble, underwood.
s.m. A cricket (cf. S. ćingaṭa).

v.n. = jhiṅgārnā, q.v. v.n. To buzz, &c. = jhan-kārnā, and ćinghāṛnā, qq.v. v.t. To cause to give forth a clinking sound to tinkle, jingle, clank, clink, ring, rattle, &c.
s.m. A steersman.
s.m. (dialec.) = jhīṅgar, q.v. s.m. Bush, bramble, underwood.
adj. Lean, thin, emaciated, bony, raw-boned.
s.m. Name of a subdivision of the Gūjar tribe.
s.f. A cowrie or shell with the top worn away or broken off; a woman with a flat nose.

ety: Prk. झीण redupl.+अओ = S. क्षीण redupl.+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Worn, decayed, worn away; thin, emaciated; threadbare; thin, fine (as cloth).
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.
s.f. = jhinjhoṭī, q.v. s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.

ety: jhīṅk˚ = Prk.झिक्क(इ) or झिक्के(इ); S. शीत्क्रीय(ते) pass. (used actively) fr. शीत्+कृ
def: v.n. To grieve, regret, repine, sigh, lament; to fret, whine, whimper; — to recommend anything seriously; — s.m. A tale of sorrow or distress, a grievance.

ety: jhīṅk˚ = Prk.झिक्क(इ) or झिक्के(इ); S. शीत्क्रीय(ते) pass. (used actively) fr. शीत्+कृ
def: v.n. To grieve, regret, repine, sigh, lament; to fret, whine, whimper; — to recommend anything seriously; — s.m. A tale of sorrow or distress, a grievance.
s.f. = jhīsī, q.v. s.f. Mist-like rain, misle, drizzle, shower.
s.f. The shrub Barleria cristata.

ety: S. क्षर+क; but cf. jhaṭak, jhatkā
def: s.f. A pull, jerk; a threat; an augury
subs: jhark-ā-jhaṛk-ī, s.f. Mutual pulling and jerking; mutual wrangling.

def: v.t. To shake, jerk; to drive away, beat off, put aside or away; to snap at, to scold, rebuke, chide, threaten, browbeat
subs: v.t To shake or jerk off; to drive away, beat off, &c.

def: v.t. To cause to be scolded, to have (one) chid, &c.
s.f. Opening, fissure, cleft, crevice, slit, crack, chink; — a small spring dug in a nālā (where the water percolates a few feet below the surface); — blight; withered or blighted grain.
s.f. = jhillī, q.v. s.f. A thin skin, a pellicle, the inner skin (of the stomach, &c.), a membrane, the omentum or caul; the inner bark (of a tree); parchment.
adj. Running in a slender stream (water, &c.), trickling; splashing.
adj. (f. -ī), Worn, thin, emaciated; thin, flimsy, badly woven (cloth).

ety: jhirjhir, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To run or flow in a small stream, to trickle.

ety: a var. form of the preceding
def: v.n. To rage, rail, storm, bluster.
s.f. Chiding, scolding; petulance, snappishness; rage, bluster.
v.t. = jhiṛaknā, q.v.; and = jhiṛakwānā, q.v. v.t. To shake, jerk; to drive away, beat off, put aside or away; to snap at, to scold, rebuke, chide, threaten, browbeat
subs: v.t To shake or jerk off; to drive away, beat off, &c.

def: s.f. Jerk; repercussion; snapping; snappishness; frowning; threat; scolding, rebuke
subs: jhiṛkiyāṅ denā, v.t. To snap at, to chide, scold, rebuke, threaten.

def: s.f. Mist-like rain, misle, drizzle, shower.
s.m. A fight, a quarrel, &c. = jūjh
s.m. A stomach. paunch; a pendulous belly; nest (of a bird, = jhūṅjh or jhoṅjh).
s.m. A paunch ( = jhojh); — a pot-bellied person, a corpulent man; — name of an inferior class of Musalmāns in the Doab, &c. (chiefly converts from Hinduism).
adj. Cracked (as a voice, sound, &c.).

s.m. A kind of grass (to which camels are very partial, and which is occasionally given as fodder to horned cattle. It grows to the height of about two feet, and is known also by the name of janglī nīl, 'wild indigo').
adj. = jhojhrā, q.v. adj. Cracked (as a voice, sound, &c.).

s.m. (in H., but) f. (in Urdū), Inclination, leaning over, deviation (from perpendicularity), deflexion; bend, incurvation; dipping (of a scale), preponderance, weight; (in Mech.) sensibility (of a balauce); breeze, current (of air), gust, blast, violent sweep (of a sudden gust), impulse (of a squall, &c.); influence of drink or intoxication, &c.); swoop (of a bird of prey); shock, impetus, a shove or push (in swinging); blow, collision; rolling, reeling, staggering
subs: s.f. An empty note or paper, a fraudulent note of hand, or cheque, or bill, &c.
(-kā, or -kī), To withstand or bear up against a gust (of wind, as a tree, &c.), to bear the shock (of), to weather (a storm); to endure (a loss)
To receive a shock or blow, to be displaced, to roll, &c.
To give a (fraudulent) twist or turn (to the beam of a scale).

def: s.m. Breeze, current (of wind), puff, gust, blast; contact, collision, blow, shock ( = jhok); swaying, swinging, swing, oscillation; fraudulent turn (of a balance, in weighing).

ety: rt. of jhok-nā, 'to throw'+iʼā = iwā = Prk. इअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. The thrower of fuel (into an oven, or a furnace), fireman ( = jhokiyā, q.v.).
s.f. The spot on which the fireman stands to feed the fire of a furnace, &c.; the mouth of a furnace.
s.m. One who supplies fuel to a furnace, a fireman ( = jhoṅkiyā; jhokā).

ety: jhok˚ = Prk. झेवक्क(इ), fr. S. क्षेप (or क्षप)+कृ
def: v.t. To throw, cast, toss; to toss down, pour down, quaff, guzzle, swill; to put (in); to supply (fuel, to a furnace or oven), to heat (an oven or a furnace); to set fire to (straw, &c.); — v.n. To sway, roll, reel, stagger; — s.m. Fuel (for heating a furnace or an oven)
subs: v.t. (intens.) To throw away (in or on, meṅ), to waste, squander (money, lives, &c.); to risk, hazard, put at stake; to set fire to (straw, &c.); to toss down, &c. ( = jhoknā).

def: v.t. To cause to incline or bend; to bring (a gun) into position (to fire), to point (a gun); to aim (at, -par), direct (speech at = jhukhānā); to bear down upon; to swoop down on.

s.m. Puckering or bagging (as of ill-made clothes), bagginess, rumple, pucker, crease; incurvation or contraction (of a limb, &c.), palsy ( = jholā) ; — a brood, hatch; a litter, farrow; a birth; a batch (of eggs); — gravy, broth, soup ( = jūsh); — a wash of gold, gilding (syn. mulammā)
subs: (-par), To put a wash of gold (on), to gild
s.m. Shaking, scuffle, squabble, altercation, quarrel, row; delay
adj. Puckered, rumpled, bagging, baggy; — having a gravy; — gilded
(-meṅ), To make baggy, to pucker, rumple, crease
v.n. To take the puckering or bagginess (out of, -meṅ-se), to remove a rumple or crease (from), to smooth; to raise a brood, to hatch; to litter; to bring forth, give birth (to).
